page: Vultr vcg-a16-96c-960g-256vram is a Cloud GPU family server designed for high-density graphical and compute workloads. The system features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor with 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs, operating at one thread per core. It is equipped with 960.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 10.0 GB of RAM per core.

The primary accelerator hardware consists of 16 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 256 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1700 GB NVMe SSD, which handles high-speed local data access. The configuration also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and the single-thread-per-core topology, which may affect workloads requiring dedicated, sustained single-core CPU performance. However, the high density of 16 GPUs and 960.0 GB of RAM within a single instance offers high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that offload processing to the GPU cluster rather than relying on dedicated host CPU execution.

This server configuration is designed for multi-user virtual desktop infrastructure, video streaming, media transcoding, and parallelized GPU-accelerated workloads that benefit from multiple physical GPU units and large system memory.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-96c-960g-256vram is a Cloud GPU family server built on the x86_64 architecture with an Intel processor. It features 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a single-thread-per-core topology under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 960.0 GB of RAM, offering 10.0 GB of memory per core. Acceleration is driven by 16 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a total of 256 GB of VRAM, complemented by 1700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for CPU-bound tasks, the high density of GPUs and system memory provides qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized graphical and compute workloads. This instance is suited for virtual desktop infrastructure, media transcoding, and GPU-accelerated streaming applications.
